raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, several unauthorized fraudulent transactions were made on my American Express credit card account. 

Earlier that day, my phone was stolen. My SIM card was not reactivated until XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>, as confirmed by the attached XXXX receipt ( XXXX XXXX XXXX location ). 

During the time the fraudulent transactions occurred, I did not have possession of my phone and my SIM card was inactive. Therefore, I could not have authorized or verified any transactions by phone. 

American Express claims that the transactions were verified by voice authorization. However, the company has refused to provide : Exact timestamps ( date, time, and time zone ) for each disputed transaction Call logs or recording confirmation The phone number used for the alleged verification Any technical evidence showing I authorized these charges Without documented proof of authorization, the company can not reasonably conclude that I approved these transactions. 

I am requesting that American Express either provide documented proof of authorization or reverse all fraudulent charges in full.
